ogTitle: Alfred Cove vs Alkimos: Climate Safety

heading: Alfred Cove vs Alkimos — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Alfred Cove, Western Australia and Alkimos, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Alfred Cove has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Alkimos (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Alfred Cove or Alkimos safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Alfred Cove has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Alkimos scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Alfred Cove scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Alkimos scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Alfred Cove or Alkimos?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Alfred Cove has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
